bullets.text: Worked on various features across the company's customer-facing web platform
bullets.gap: vague verb ('worked on'), no domain/keyword signal, no metric — reframe toward the backend/API work that actually sits behind these features

bullets.text: Built and maintained React components for the user dashboard and account settings pages
bullets.gap: frontend (React UI) work; off-domain for a backend payments role — demote or drop

bullets.text: Helped improve API response times for several slow endpoints
bullets.gap: real backend/performance signal but buried: 'helped' hedges ownership, no numbers, no mention of throughput/latency scale

bullets.text: Integrated a third-party payments provider (Stripe) into the checkout flow
bullets.gap: direct payments-domain experience — the single most JD-relevant line, but undersold as a one-off 'integration'; surface payment-flow + reliability ownership

bullets.text: Wrote and maintained backend endpoints in Python (Flask) backing the dashboard
bullets.gap: hits two priority keywords (backend + Python) but reads as routine CRUD — quantify scope/traffic and name it service work

bullets.text: Participated in code reviews, sprint planning, and other agile ceremonies
bullets.gap: generic process line; only worth keeping if compressed into one delivery-practices bullet

bullets.text: Fixed bugs reported by customers and triaged incoming support tickets
bullets.gap: could map to reliability / incident response if tied to production issues and root-cause fixes rather than ticket triage

bullets.text: Developed internal tools and services using Python
bullets.gap: Python is a high-priority keyword and 'services' is backend — surface it harder; specify these were backend services, not one-off scripts

bullets.text: Wrote scripts to automate nightly data-processing and reporting jobs
bullets.gap: data-processing-at-volume is adjacent to high-throughput; quantify the record volume and time saved

bullets.text: Maintained and tuned a production MySQL database backing the analytics product
bullets.gap: MySQL is transferable to the must-have PostgreSQL (relational DB, query tuning) — name the transferable skill honestly, don't claim Postgres

bullets.text: Built a small REST API to expose reporting data to other internal teams
bullets.gap: backend/API ownership but 'small' undersells it; describe the API's consumers and reliability/versioning concerns

bullets.text: Assisted senior engineers with backend tasks and on-call investigations
bullets.gap: 'assisted' undersells; backend is the must-have headline and on-call is a JD responsibility — own the contribution

bullets.text: Added logging and basic dashboards to help debug a recurring data pipeline issue
bullets.gap: logging + dashboards is exactly observability/monitoring, but framed as a one-off fix — generalize it into an observability practice and tie to reduced incidents

bullets.text: Built responsive marketing websites for small-business clients using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript
bullets.gap: frontend / marketing-site work at an early role; off-domain for backend payments — drop

bullets.text: Customized WordPress themes and plugins to client specifications
bullets.gap: WordPress/CMS theming; no backend or payments relevance — drop

bullets.text: Optimized page load times and improved Lighthouse scores for client sites
bullets.gap: client-side performance (Lighthouse) is a different discipline from backend throughput — drop, even though it superficially says 'optimized'

bullets.text: Coordinated with designers to translate Figma mockups into pixel-perfect pages
Set up basic Google Analytics tracking and SEO metadata for launched sites
bullets.gap: marketing analytics / SEO; no engineering signal for this JD — drop

must_have_covered: backend engineering (partial — Python/Flask endpoints, internal services, reporting API)
PostgreSQL (transferable via production MySQL tuning, not literal)
must_have_missing: Go or Java (Python only — Python is accepted, so soft-blocker not hard)

transferable: Python (held by candidate; JD accepts Python) — strongest bridge to backend engineering
Production MySQL tuning → PostgreSQL (relational DB, query performance)
API response-time + Flask backend work → high-throughput / service-performance adjacency
Nightly data-processing of large record volumes → high-volume processing adjacency
Logging + dashboards for a pipeline issue → observability / monitoring
Stripe checkout integration → genuine payments-domain exposure (nice-to-have, real)
On-call investigation experience → the JD's on-call rotation requirement
profile_note: Candidate reads as a full-stack generalist (~6 yrs) who leans frontend on the surface — the entire earliest role (Pixel & Co) and one current-role bullet are frontend/marketing and irrelevant here. But there is real, buried backend signal at both NovaSoft and Brightwave (Python/Flask services, a reporting REST API, MySQL tuning, observability work) plus genuine payments exposure via the Stripe integration. Not a literal keyword match on the hard must-haves (Go/Java/Kafka/distributed systems) — treat as a transferable-skills case: surface the Python + DB + API + observability + payments bridges, and be honest that distributed systems and Kafka are real gaps.
